entries.summary: Created canonical record files for paper tracking, inbox triage, topic focus, and future update-paper workflow.
Set Temporal hallucination in video understanding as the primary topic and added related seed papers and search terms.
Added paper-library folders, manifest generation, download script, and logs so future chats can continue PDF/download work from files instead of chat memory.
Applied minimal Chinese fixes to high-frequency navigation, taskboard, inbox, and detail UI copy while keeping core research terms in English when helpful.

Updated the paper downloader to resolve CVF and OpenReview PDF targets directly, downloaded the remaining paper-library PDFs successfully, and left only trtv as a manual follow-up due to an OpenReview 403 response.
Confirmed the manually supplied trtv PDF is in paper-library/pdfs, refreshed the download report so every manifest paper is now present on disk, and removed the obsolete manual follow-up note.
Added notes/after-summary-workflow.md to define the repeatable post-summary workflow for evidence checks, figure handling, website quick-read buttons, decision framing, and records updates.
Added paper-insights data and website quick-understand support so selected papers can show a one-minute brief, structured bullets, and future figure slots directly inside the paper detail view.

Completed the first insights batch for mvbench, timechat, moviechat, mlvu, longvideobench, lvhaystack, vited, egotempo, seq2time, step, videocomp, and groundmore; installed Poppler locally and generated preview figures for the available insight papers.
Filled site/paper-insights.json for every paper in the manifest so all tracked papers now have quick-understand metadata for the website.
Added scripts to generate first-page preview images from PDFs and populated website figure paths so every paper insight now has a preview image.
Added full summary markdown files for seq2time, step, videocomp, groundmore, univtg, protege, guidance, and scanonce.
Filled summary markdown files for every paper in the manifest so the paper library now has complete summaries, website insights, and preview figures for all tracked papers.

Reworked the website paper-reading flow so Quick understand is more informative and Detailed understand now opens a separate lesson window backed by site/paper-lessons.json and site/lessons/* instead of the older in-page modal flow.

Stabilized the Detailed understand floating paper viewer: it is now a top-layer movable and resizable preview window with previous/next page navigation, zoom, and non-blocking seminar reading workflow.
Restyled the main site homepage/dashboard to feel like the lesson seminar deck in a blue version while keeping the lesson pages themselves on their own visual system.
Unified all 28 papers under the shared seminar-style Detailed understand deck, removed TempCompass-only branching, and confirmed the lesson routes for multiple papers still load successfully.

Polished the seminar content for all 28 papers in site/paper-lessons.json so thesis, problem framing, method, results, takeaways, discussion prompts, and summary notes read like a PhD seminar report rather than generic summaries.

Changed lesson slide titles and section headers from teaching-meta wording to seminar-presentation wording such as Research Problem, Motivation and Framing, Method Overview, Experimental Findings, and Key Takeaways.

Added Video-MME to the paper library, Week 1 roadmap, website quick-understand content, seminar lesson deck, local PDF library, and preview assets so it can be compared directly against TempCompass and MVBench.
